statement: The cabinet of session 157 comprises ministers from the three coalition parties. Samfylkingin: Kristrún Frostadóttir (forsætisráðherra / Prime Minister), Alma D. Möller (heilbrigðisráðherra / Minister of Health), Jóhann Páll Jóhannsson (umhverfis-, orku- og loftslagsráðherra / Minister of Environment, Energy and Climate), Logi Einarsson (menningar-, nýsköpunar- og háskólaráðherra / Minister of Culture, Innovation and Higher Education; also ráðherra norrænna samstarfsmála / Minister for Nordic Cooperation). Viðreisn: Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir (utanríkisráðherra / Foreign Minister), Daði Már Kristófersson (fjármála- og efnahagsráðherra / Minister of Finance and Economic Affairs), Hanna Katrín Friðriksson (atvinnuvegaráðherra / Minister of Industries), Þorbjörg Sigríður Gunnlaugsdóttir (dómsmálaráðherra / Minister of Justice). Flokkur fólksins: Eyjólfur Ármannsson (innviðaráðherra / Minister of Infrastructure), Inga Sæland (félags- og húsnæðismálaráðherra / Minister of Social Affairs and Housing), Guðmundur Ingi Kristinsson (mennta- og barnamálaráðherra / Minister of Education and Children), Ragnar Þór Ingólfsson (félags- og húsnæðismálaráðherra / Minister of Social Affairs and Housing).
caveats: Cabinet composition may change with reshuffles. Some ministers hold dual portfolios. Daði Már Kristófersson and Guðmundur Ingi Kristinsson are not sitting MPs (outside ministers).

related_entries.statement: The current Icelandic government is a coalition of Samfylkingin (Social Democrats, 13 seats), Viðreisn (Reform Party, 11 seats), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party, 8 seats), holding 32 of 63 Althingi seats. The Prime Minister is Kristrún Frostadóttir (Samfylkingin). This is the first government coalition where both explicitly pro-EU parties (Samfylkingin and Viðreisn) serve together, which has been cited as a factor in advancing the EU referendum.

Viðreisn (Reform Party) is led by Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir, who serves as utanríkisráðherra (Foreign Minister) in the current government. She is an MP for Suðvesturkjördæmi. Viðreisn was founded in 2014 (originally as a political movement, became a party in 2016) with a pro-EU platform as a core policy. The party holds 11 Althingi seats and consistently advocates for completing EU accession negotiations. Other prominent Viðreisn MPs include Pawel Bartoszek (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Jón Gnarr (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), and Hanna Katrín Friðriksson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður, also Minister of Industries).

citations.report_title: Trump sagður beita sér í ESB-umræðunni hér á landi

claims.claim_text: Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir er utanríkisráðherra Íslands.
